content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 ,J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Robin Rauch 
Treasurer of the City of Colusa 
433 4th Street 
Colusa, CA 95932 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-163 
Dear Ms. Rauch: 
September 6, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding your duties as Treasurer of the City 
of Colusa, under the gift provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
If a third party pays a debt that you have contracted to pay, is this a gift to you subject to 
reporting on your Statement of Economic Interest (Form 700)? 
CONCLUSION 
Since the payment complies with the exception in Regulation 18942(a)( 19), the payment 
would not be a gift subject to reporting on your Form 700. 
FACTS 
You are the current Elected Treasurer of the City of Colusa. In your role as treasurer, you 
stated that: 
1. You do not make any decisions as to vendors. 
2. You do not vote on the City Council. 
1 The Political Refonn Act 1s contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicaled. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Conumssion are contained in Sections 181 IO through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless other.vise indicated. 


File No. A-16-t 63 
Page No. 2 
3. You do not have any influence over the investment decisions for the city since this is the 
role of the City Council. 
4. You stated that your only influence over the City council would be that of any other non­
elected regular citizen. 
A friend of yours, Hector, recently had some legal issues which needed to be addressed. 
You stated that Hector attends the same church that you attend. Hector was not in a position to pay 
for legal services. You agreed to be responsible for the cost of the legal services that Hector 
received. The legal matter has now been completed and you just received an accounting from the 
attorney as to the balance owed for her legal services. 
On that accounting was an indication that payments have been made by a third party, Debra 
Tripp, president of Solace Counseling Marriage and Family Therapy. Ms. Tripp is also a member of 
the church and felt the need for Hector to receive the legal services. Ms. Tripp has made various 
payments, directly to the attorney, to support Hector's case. Her payments aggregate to S900. You 
stated that she never intended her payments to be a gift to you. According to a communication you 
receive from Ms. Tripp, she stated: 
"I am sorry to hear that my financial support of my friend Hector Franco 
has created a potential problem .... 
"I made contributions directly to Ann Block, who I presumed was 
Hector's attorney. I made them from my business (S Corp) account .... 
"Although I do work in Colusa two days per week, the main office is in 
Sacramento. I can't think of any reason or manner in which I would be able to 
influence you in your duties, or you would be able to benefit me in my practice." 
According to the e-mail you attached from Hector's attorney, Ann Block: 
"The fee agreement lists clients as Hector, you and Hector Jose. Only you 
signed it, as Hector was in detention at the time and Hector Jose was unable to 
come to my office that day, as I recall, just his wife. However, all the client work 
was done for Hector, not for you. Nothing in fact was done for you. 
"Debra has always indicated that her checks were for Hector's 
representation, not for you, if that helps .... not sure that Debra's payments had 
anything to do with you personally, other than the fact that you agreed to be 
responsible for the legal fees for Hector, and then Debra decided to help as well!" 
On August 29, 2016, you supplemented your facts and stated that Ms. Tripp is the 
sole owner of her business. 
ANALYSIS 
The tenn "gift" is defined in Section 82028(a) as: 


File No. A-16-163 
Page No. 3 
"Any payment that confers a personal benefit on the recipient, to the 
extent that consideration of equal or !,'feater value is not received and includes a 
rebate or discount in the price of anything of value unless the rebate or discount is 
made in the regular course of business to members of the public without regard to 
official status." 
In an effort to reduce improper influences on public officials, the Act regulates the receipt of 
gifts by public officials in three ways: 
• 
First, the Act places limitations on the acceptance of gifts by certain public officials. The 
current limit is $460 from a single source in a calendar year. (Section 89503; Regulation 
18940.2.) This gift limit applies to, among others, all individuals in positions listed in 
Section 87200. (Section 89503.) Section 87200 includes city treasurers. 
• Secondly, the Act imposes reporting obligations on certain public officials, requiring that 
any gifts that aggregate to $50 or more from the same source received during the calendar 
year arc disclosed on the officials' statements of economic interest. Reporting requirements 
apply to all officials listed in Section 87200. 
• Finally, the Act prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence the outcome of a governmental decision involving the donor 
of a gift or gifts with an ag!,'fegate value of $460 or more provided to, received by, or 
promised to the official within the 12 months prior to the date the decision is made. 
(Sections 87100, 87103(e), Regulations 18700, 18703.4.) 
The payment of a debt that you are contractually obligated to pay, without your provision of 
consideration of equal or greater value, confers a personal benefit on you and absent an exception, 
would be considered a gift. 
Exceptions 
You asked specifically about the exception in Regulation l 8942(a)( I 9) that provides: 
"Any other payment not identified above, that would otherwise meet the 
definition of gift, where the payment is made by an individual who is not a 
lobbyist registered to lobby the official's agency, where it is clear that the gift was 
made because of an existing personal or business relationship unrelated to the 
official's position and there is no evidence whatsoever at the time the gift is made 
that the official makes or participates in the type of governmental decisions that 
may have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the individual who 
would othenvise be the source of the gift." 
The language of the exception is limited to individual donors and not business donors. 
However, while the "source" of the funds under your facts is an S-corporation and not an 
individual, your facts demonstrate that the gift was not made by the business, but by Ms. Tripp, 
based on her relationship to Hector and who merely chose to use business funds to make the gift. 


File No. A-16-163 
Page No. 4 
Since all the remaining requirements of the exception are met, the exception applies and the 
payments would not be considered a reportable gift. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
t 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
( 
John W. Wallace 
Assistant General Counsel 
Legal Division
